claim: Minimalist prompt chaining with zero libraries (just string templating and JSON parsing) can solve 80% of sequential prompt chain use cases
actionable: Build prompt chains using simple string replacement and JSON parsing instead of heavyweight frameworks like LangChain.
source_title: When to use Prompt Chains. DITCHING LangChain. ALL HAIL Claude 3.5 Sonnet

claim: Breaking complex tasks into separate sequential prompts (instead of one large prompt) improves LLM performance by allowing it to reason on smaller problems one at a time
actionable: Split multi-step tasks into a chain of focused prompts rather than combining them into a single complex prompt.
source_title: When to use Prompt Chains. DITCHING LangChain. ALL HAIL Claude 3.5 Sonnet

claim: Use prompt chains when: (1) task is too complex for single prompt, (2) need to maximize performance/reduce errors, (3) subsequent prompts depend on previous outputs, (4) need adaptive workflow with conditional logic
actionable: Before building a prompt chain, verify at least one of these four conditions applies to your use case.
source_title: When to use Prompt Chains. DITCHING LangChain. ALL HAIL Claude 3.5 Sonnet

claim: LLM libraries like LangChain and AutoGen become hard to finish once complexity increases - you need to dig into code to understand what they're actually doing
actionable: Stay close to raw prompts and avoid libraries until you encounter problems that absolutely require abstraction.
source_title: When to use Prompt Chains. DITCHING LangChain. ALL HAIL Claude 3.5 Sonnet

claim: LLM libraries introduce unnecessary abstractions and premature opinions about how to do things, which become outdated as model capabilities and best practices evolve
actionable: Question framework decisions and maintain low-abstraction implementations that adapt quickly to new models.
source_title: When to use Prompt Chains. DITCHING LangChain. ALL HAIL Claude 3.5 Sonnet

claim: The core value of generative AI is the prompt itself, and abstractions often hide this critical element from developers
actionable: Keep prompts visible and under your direct control; don't let libraries abstract them away.
source_title: When to use Prompt Chains. DITCHING LangChain. ALL HAIL Claude 3.5 Sonnet

claim: Claude 3.5 Sonnet is beating GPT-4 on nearly every benchmark with significantly better performance
actionable: Prioritize Claude 3.5 Sonnet over GPT-4 for new projects when cost and latency matter.
source_title: When to use Prompt Chains. DITCHING LangChain. ALL HAIL Claude 3.5 Sonnet

claim: Referencing outputs from previous prompts in a chain requires structured notation (like going back N prompts) that can be built into a minimal chainable class
actionable: Implement output references in prompt chains using consistent syntax (e.g., prev_1, prev_2) to access earlier results.
source_title: When to use Prompt Chains. DITCHING LangChain. ALL HAIL Claude 3.5 Sonnet

claim: Agentic workflows are built by starting with good prompt chains and building on top of that foundation
actionable: Master prompt chains before attempting to build agentic systems; chains are the building block.
source_title: When to use Prompt Chains. DITCHING LangChain. ALL HAIL Claude 3.5 Sonnet
